name: Optimize Go Solana vanity grinder on Apple Silicon

description: Add benchmark mode and batched attempt accounting; best 5s run at 64 threads

asi.hypothesis: Batching global attempt counter updates and adding duration benchmark reduces atomic contention, especially with high thread counts; math shows optimal threads should be near CPU count, with extra goroutines giving only small gains.
asi.result: Throughput is flat from 16-512 threads around 284-286k keys/sec; 512 threads is not faster because only 8 CPUs execute and scheduling overhead offsets extra work.
asi.probability_math: For prefix puter, search space is 58^5=656,356,768. Mean is one full space, p50≈455M attempts, avg-to-first per README convention≈328M attempts, p95≈1.97B attempts. At 286k/s p50≈26.5m and p95≈1.9h.

description: Avoid Base58 address string allocation on non-matching attempts

asi.hypothesis: For long vanity searches nearly all attempts miss, so checking Base58 prefix/suffix directly from digit buffers should reduce allocations and improve sustained throughput.
asi.result: Small improvement over baseline best 286111 to 287844 keys/sec in repeated benchmark; earlier same code 128-thread sample reached 288434. Ed25519 key generation appears dominant.
asi.math_note: At 287844 keys/sec, puter prefix p50≈26.4 minutes and p95≈1.9 hours.
asi.next_action_hint: Profile key generation path and try avoiding ed25519.PrivateKey allocation/public interface call.

description: Direct Ed25519 public-key math attempt failed to build due unused import

asi.hypothesis: Avoid ed25519.NewKeyFromSeed private-key allocation and compute public keys directly with edwards25519 scalar-base multiplication.
asi.rollback_reason: Build failed because crypto/ed25519 import remained unused after refactor.
asi.next_action_hint: Remove unused import and rerun the direct public-key benchmark.

description: Use real puter-prefix workload and periodic counter flush for accurate progress

asi.hypothesis: Benchmarking with the real target prefix puter and flushing local counters every 16384 attempts will provide accurate progress with negligible throughput cost.
asi.result: Best puter workload throughput was 285316 keys/sec at 16 threads, slightly below existing 287844 best, so discard for optimization tracking. Progress counters now work but this code change is not worth keeping as an optimization result.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve versus current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue using --prefix puter. Next try direct Ed25519 public key computation without private-key allocation, or profile CPU hotspots.
asi.math: For puter, search space=58^5=656356768; p50≈454951843 attempts. At 285316/s p50≈26.6 min, p95≈1.91 h.

description: Direct edwards25519 public-key derivation for puter workload

asi.hypothesis: Using edwards25519 scalar-base multiplication directly from seed will avoid ed25519.PrivateKey allocation and interface Public() overhead for non-matches.
asi.result: Worse on puter workload. The standard library ed25519 path is faster on Apple Silicon/Go for this workload, likely due optimized implementation and less dependency overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric fell to 280425 keys/sec versus best 287844/286111 baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use filippo direct scalar path. Focus on reducing Base58 work using prefix-specific numeric math or test deterministic counter seed mode to reduce random overhead.
asi.math: At 280425/s, puter p50≈27.0 min; slower than current best.

description: Prefix-only Base58 match fast path for puter workload

asi.hypothesis: Since the target is exactly prefix puter with no suffix, a prefix-only Base58 check can remove generic suffix and charAt closure overhead.
asi.result: Matched current range but did not clearly beat prior best 287844; 512-thread sample reached 287593 and 16 threads 287488.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve over current best puter-compatible result.
asi.next_action_hint: Try seed generation changes. Random entropy batching may still cost; deterministic passphrase/counter mode could show upper bound for non-random seed generation.
asi.math: At ~287.6k/s, puter p50≈26.4 min and p95≈1.9 h.

description: Compare random entropy pool versus deterministic SHA256 seed stream on puter

asi.hypothesis: If random seed generation is a bottleneck, deterministic SHA256(passphrase||counter) seed generation should outperform crypto/rand batched pool; this provides a math-guided upper bound for seed-generation alternatives.
asi.result: No improvement. Random and deterministic modes were both around 280-282k/s; seed generation is not the dominant cost. Ed25519 public-key generation remains dominant.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve versus best.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on Ed25519 implementation/runtime tuning, not seed generation. Try GOMAXPROCS/CPU affinity-like thread counts and longer tests for stable best setting.
asi.math: A seed generator improvement cannot move total throughput much if replacing random with SHA256 barely changes keys/sec.

description: Test GOMAXPROCS and thread-count combinations for puter workload

asi.hypothesis: On an Apple Silicon Mac with 4 performance and 4 efficiency cores, GOMAXPROCS/runtime scheduling might materially affect throughput more than worker goroutine count.
asi.result: Best was GOMAXPROCS=8 with 8 threads at 285952 keys/sec; still below current best. GOMAXPROCS=4 underuses CPU (~213k/s) and GOMAXPROCS=6 is only ~255k/s. 512 threads remains worse than 8-64 threads.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement versus best.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch strategies: Go CPU implementation has plateaued around 285-288k/s. Further gains likely require a faster Ed25519 implementation, Rust/Solana grinder, GPU, or distributed/multi-machine search.
asi.math: At 286k/s, puter p50 is ~26.5 minutes and p95 is ~1.9 hours; 2x speedup would only come from a different keygen backend, not goroutine tuning.
